perturbation.original_text: 1. The term "Confidential Information" shall mean any information and data of a confidential nature belonging to the disclosing party, its affiliates and/or licensors ("Discloser"), including without limitation, proprietary, technical, developmental, marketing, sales, operating, financial, performance, cost, business and process information and plans, software, and computer programming techniques which are disclosed or made available pursuant to this Agreement in connection with the Evaluation.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. The term "Confidential Information" shall mean any information and data of a confidential nature belonging to the disclosing party, its affiliates and/or licensors ("Discloser"), including only financial information. This definition is limited to financial data exclusively, anything other information won't be considered confidential.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include 'only financial information', it directly contradicts the later inclusion of 'technical, developmental, marketing, sales, operating, performance, cost, business and process information and plans, software, and computer programming techniques' as confidential. This creates uncertainty as to which types of information are protected under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. The term "Confidential Information" shall mean any information and data of a confidential nature belonging to the disclosing party, its affiliates and/or licensors ("Discloser"), including only financial information. This definition is limited to financial data exclusively, anything other information won't be considered confidential.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly. The changed text narrows the definition to 'only financial information'. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text. Based on the explanation provided, this creates a clear contradiction because it limits the scope of what is considered confidential compared to the original and any other sections relying on the original broader definition.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Recipient hereby agrees that the Confidential Information will be used by it solely for the purposes of discussions with Discloser relating to the Evaluation and as necessary to fulfill any obligations Recipient may have to Discloser pursuant to any agreements the parties may enter into or otherwise. Recipient agrees not to disclose the Confidential Information of the other party, in any form, to any third party except as contemplated herein. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidential nature of the Confidential Information; provided however, that any such Confidential Information may be disclosed to its employees who need access to such <PAGE> information for such purposes and are made aware of and agree to be bound by the confidentiality obligations contained herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Recipient hereby agrees that the Confidential Information will be used by it solely for the purposes of discussions with Discloser relating to the Evaluation and as necessary to fulfill any obligations Recipient may have to Discloser pursuant to any agreements the parties may enter into or otherwise. Recipient agrees not to disclose the Confidential Information of the other party, in any form, to any third party except as contemplated herein. Recipient is not required to maintain the confidential nature of the Confidential Information; however, any such Confidential Information may be disclosed to its employees who need access to such <PAGE> information for such purposes.
perturbation.explanation: By stating 'Recipient is not required to maintain the confidential nature of the Confidential Information', this directly contradicts the previous sentence stating that the recipient agrees to maintain the confidential nature of the confidential information. This introduces a contradiction regarding the core obligation of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Recipient hereby agrees that the Confidential Information will be used by it solely for the purposes of discussions with Discloser relating to the Evaluation and as necessary to fulfill any obligations Recipient may have to Discloser pursuant to any agreements the parties may enter into or otherwise. Recipient agrees not to disclose the Confidential Information of the other party, in any form, to any third party except as contemplated herein. Recipient is not required to maintain the confidential nature of the Confidential Information; however, any such Confidential Information may be disclosed to its employees who need access to such <PAGE> information for such purposes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself and the original intent. The original text states 'Recipient agrees to maintain the confidential nature of the Confidential Information', while the changed text states 'Recipient is not required to maintain the confidential nature of the Confidential Information'. The contradiction is very clear and appears in the same section.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Upon termination of the Evaluation of the parties' relationship with respect thereto, and upon Discloser's request, Recipient shall return to Discloser all materials reflecting or containing any of Discloser's Confidential Information and shall not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Upon termination of the Evaluation of the parties' relationship with respect thereto, the Recipient is not obligated to return to Discloser all materials reflecting or containing any of Discloser's Confidential Information, but shall retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires return of confidential information upon termination. The changed text states the Recipient is not obligated to return confidential information and must retain copies, directly contradicting the prior requirement and creating confusion about the obligations after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Upon termination of the Evaluation of the parties' relationship with respect thereto, the Recipient is not obligated to return to Discloser all materials reflecting or containing any of Discloser's Confidential Information, but shall retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the return of confidential information upon termination. The changed text explicitly states the Recipient is not obligated to return confidential information and must retain copies. This directly contradicts the requirement established in the original text, as described in the Perturbation Explanation, causing a structural flaw that makes the contractual obligations unclear, and creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which also says that Recipient is not obligated to return confidential information and must retain copies.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Each party acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure or use of the other party's Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm to such other party. Each party agrees that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach by it of this Agreement and that the non-breaching party shall be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Each party acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure or use of the other party's Confidential Information may not cause irreparable harm to such other party. Each party agrees that money damages are a sufficient remedy for any breach by it of this Agreement and that the non-breaching party is not entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.explanation: The original text claims unauthorized disclosure may cause irreparable harm, making money damages insufficient and allowing for equitable relief. The changed text states that unauthorized disclosure may not cause irreparable harm, that money damages are sufficient, and equitable relief is not available, directly contradicting the original assessment of potential harm and remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Each party acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure or use of the other party's Confidential Information may not cause irreparable harm to such other party. Each party agrees that money damages are a sufficient remedy for any breach by it of this Agreement and that the non-breaching party is not entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that unauthorized disclosure may cause irreparable harm, making money damages insufficient and allowing for equitable relief. The changed text asserts the opposite: that unauthorized disclosure may not cause irreparable harm, money damages are sufficient, and equitable relief is not available. This directly contradicts the original assessment of potential harm and remedies. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, which means that the changed text contradicts itself. Therefore, this creates a strong contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: 7. The parties hereto shall not be obligated to compensate each other for disclosure of any information under this Agreement and agree that no warranties of any kind are given with respect to such information, as well as any use thereof. It is understood that no patent, copyright, trademark or other proprietary right or license is granted by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. The parties hereto shall be obligated to compensate each other for disclosure of any information under this Agreement and agree that full warranties of all kind are given with respect to such information, as well as any use thereof. It is understood that no patent, copyright, trademark or other proprietary right or license is granted by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that parties are not obligated to compensate each other, and no warranties are given. By changing this to obligating compensation and giving full warranties, this completely contradicts the initial clause regarding liability and assurances related to the disclosed information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. The parties hereto shall be obligated to compensate each other for disclosure of any information under this Agreement and agree that full warranties of all kind are given with respect to such information, as well as any use thereof. It is understood that no patent, copyright, trademark or other proprietary right or license is granted by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 7 directly contradicts the original text by changing 'not be obligated to compensate' to 'be obligated to compensate' and 'no warranties' to 'full warranties'. The contradicted text provided is the same as the changed text, indicating that this change introduces a direct contradiction within the document regarding compensation and warranties for disclosed information.
